invariant_definition: All-pervading. The *jīva*'s size is one of the principal Vedāntic disagreements: Advaita holds the *jīva* (qua *ātman*) is *vibhu* like *brahman*; Viśiṣṭādvaita and Dvaita hold the *jīva* is *aṇu* (atomic). The opposition is not merely terminological — it determines whether the multiplicity of *jīva*s is real (V, D — *aṇu*) or illusory (A — many appearances of one *vibhu*). *Brahma-Sūtra* 2.3.18 (*aṇur ātmā*) is the locus of dispute.

per_school.definition: *Jīva* qua *ātman* is *vibhu* like *brahman*; the appearance of localization is *upādhi*-borne. *Brahma-Sūtra* 2.3.18 is read as referring to the *upādhi* (subtle body), not the *ātman* itself.
